steel_price_impact: Immaterial - prices lower in Q1 2025 vs prior year; impact diminished by long supply chain and small raw material cost proportion
energy_price_impact: Immaterial - energy prices below prior year period; small direct fuel cost and raw material cost proportion
fx_exposure: Primary exposures: Canadian dollar and Mexican peso against U.S. dollar; immaterial impact due to local currency transaction practices
interest_rate_sensitivity: Floating rate debt under Credit Facility; relatively small debt levels; 100bp increase = $0.1 million additional interest expense
hedging_policy: Company has not historically hedged foreign currency or interest rate risks due to immaterial historical impacts and relatively small debt levels

tariff_impact: Immaterial in first six months of 2025; however, exposure may become more impactful in subsequent quarters as lower tariff inventory is depleted and replaced with inventory subject to new and expanded tariffs. Current U.S. administration has implemented tariffs and proposed additional tariffs that may increase cost and scarcity of products.
steel_price_impact: Immaterial in first six months of 2025. Steel prices were lower than prior year period. Impact diminished by long supply chain (changes take multiple quarters to reflect) and raw material cost being smaller part of total product value.
energy_price_impact: Immaterial in first six months of 2025. Energy prices below prior year period. Direct fuel consumption is relatively small cost. Fossil fuels as raw material feedstock also immaterial due to small proportion of total product value.
fx_exposure: Primary exposures: Canadian dollar and Mexican peso against U.S. dollar. Changes historically immaterial due to some transactions conducted in U.S. dollars and foreign operations having sales and expenses denominated in local currency. Dollar strengthened in first six months of 2025 but effect on net income was immaterial.
interest_rate_sensitivity: Floating rate debt under Credit Facility. Debt levels relatively small. Company has not historically used interest rate swap arrangements. 100 basis point increase would result in approximately $0.3 million additional interest expense.
hedging_policy: Company has not historically hedged foreign currency or interest rate risks due to immaterial historical impacts and relatively small debt levels. No interest rate swap arrangements used.
